info.name: 017 FILTER_REGISTRY_ENTITIES
info.description: 017 – Filter Registry Entities. Covers restaurants, plates, and pickups filter params registered in §3.2. Flow: (1) Login as admin, (2) Restaurants – eq (city, kitchen_day), in (cuisine), ilike search, (3) Plates – eq status, eq restaurant_id, eq market_id, date range, (4) Pickups – eq status, eq market_id, timestamptz window range. Run after DB has seeded test data. Uses {{baseUrl}} and {{adminAuthToken}}.

item.name: GET /plates/enriched – no filter (baseline, stores count for row-inflation guard)

exec: // Store baseline count so the row-inflation guard test below can compare.

item.name: GET /plates/enriched – filter by service date range (pickup_date gte/lte via psi join)

exec: pm.test('Row count does not exceed unfiltered baseline (no row inflation from psi join)', function () {
// A date filter can only reduce (or equal) the unfiltered count — it cannot
// inflate it. If distinct=True is working correctly, the filtered result is a
// subset of the unfiltered result. This guards against the 1:N join inflating
// plate rows when plate_selection_info has multiple reservations per plate.

item.name: GET /plate-pickup/enriched – filter by expected range (gte/lte timestamptz)

item.name: Status Context Guards (plate + plan)
item.description: Verifies that plate and plan status filters are scoped to their context. Out-of-context values (e.g. processed, which is billing-only) must return 400. In-context values (active) must return 200.
